faqs.question: What pantry pests are common in Maspeth homes?
faqs.answer: Maspeth residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, saw-toothed grain beetles, flour beetles, and rice weevils. These pests infest cereals, grains, flour, dried fruits, nuts, and other stored food products. They can enter homes through contaminated groceries or find their way through small openings from neighboring units in multi-family buildings.

faqs.question: How long does pantry pest treatment take in Maspeth?
faqs.answer: Most pantry pest treatments in Maspeth homes take 1-2 hours, depending on the size of your kitchen and the extent of infestation. This includes inspection, removal of infested items, treatment of affected areas, and preventive measures. We recommend a follow-up visit within 2-3 weeks to ensure complete elimination and address any remaining issues.

faqs.question: Do I need to throw away all my pantry food?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. During our inspection, we'll identify which items are infested and which are safe. Unopened items in undamaged packaging are typically safe to keep. We'll guide you through proper inspection techniques and recommend storing susceptible items in airtight containers. Our goal is to eliminate the pests while minimizing food waste and disruption to your household.

faqs.question: Are pantry pest treatments safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products specifically designed for food storage areas that are safe when applied correctly. Our treatments focus on targeted application, pheromone traps, and integrated pest management strategies that minimize chemical use. We'll provide specific instructions for any temporary precautions, but most treatments allow you to return to normal kitchen use within a few hours.
